name: The heart of the Catalan Pyrenees – La Fera gravel
name_original: El corazón del Pirineo catalán — La Fera gravel
description:
The great La Fera crossing takes you on a comprehensive tour of the Catalan Pyrenees. The route not only covers the great natural heritage that is the Aigüestortes i Estany de Sant Maurici National Park and the Alt Pirineu Natural Park, but also immerses you in living mountains with ancient livestock traditions and culture.

The route is circular from the Pobla de Segur train station, but due to its length, you can start it where it suits you best and shorten it thanks to the variants (https://feragravel.com/) and links it has. We recommend doing this Route between the end of June and the beginning of October, when there is more guarantee of good weather and that the high areas are clear of snow.

We also recommend that you carry out prior maintenance on your mount and that you bring tools and spare parts. Also, bring enough food and drink, as there are long stretches through high mountains and remote places.

The Fera covers both sides of the Pyrenees and runs through the valleys of the Noguera Ribagorçana, Noguera Pallaresa, Garona and Segre rivers. Its landscapes are home to an enormous cultural heritage whose traditions have been cultivated in this mountain range for millennia. Remote mountain huts, high mountain meadows and villages where time stood still centuries ago give meaning to the word bucolic. This journey is one of those where, when you return, you are not the same person who set out.
region_intro: Gravel bike trails around La Pobla De Segur navigate a diverse landscape in the Lleida Pyrenees, characterized by the Noguera Pallaresa River, the Sant Antoni Reservoir, and the dramatic Collegats Gorge. The terrain includes agricultural plains, dense forests, and rugged mountain paths, with many routes featuring unpaved segments suitable for gravel biking. The region's topography, including the Boumort mountain range and the red cliffs of Rocs de Queralt, provides varied elevation and scenic viewpoints. Best gravel bike trails around La Pobla De Segur The most popular gravel bike trail is Via Verda Pallaresa, a 19.9 miles (32.0 km) trail that takes 2 hours 45 minutes to complete, following the Noguera Pallaresa River. Another top favourite among local gravel bikers is La Coma Trail – View of Pantano de San Antonio loop from la Pobla de Segur, a difficult 21.5 miles (34.7 km) path. This route offers views of the Sant Antoni Reservoir and surrounding hills. Local gravel bikers also love the Bartotrail – La Coma Trail loop from Acadèmia General Bàsica de Suboficials, a 41.1 miles (66.1 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 6 hours 21 minutes. Gravel biking around La Pobla De Segur is defined by river valleys, reservoir views, and mountain paths, offering options for moderate to difficult ability levels. The routes in La Pobla De Segur are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 14 reviews. More than 75 gravel bikers have used komoot to explore La Pobla De Segur's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many gravel bike trails are available in La Pobla De Segur?
A: La Pobla De Segur offers a diverse network of over 8 gravel bike trails, catering to various skill levels. These routes explore the unique landscapes of the Lleida Pyrenees, from river valleys to mountain paths.

Q: Are there gravel bike trails suitable for beginners in La Pobla De Segur?
A: While many routes in the region are considered moderate to difficult, the area's diverse terrain means there are segments suitable for those newer to gravel biking. For a more accessible experience, consider routes that follow river paths or agricultural plains, which tend to have less challenging ascents. Always check the route details for elevation and surface type before you go.

Q: What are some challenging gravel routes for experienced riders in La Pobla De Segur?
A: Experienced gravel bikers looking for a challenge can explore routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40010131" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bartotrail – La Coma Trail loop from Acadèmia General Bàsica de Suboficials&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 41.1-mile (66.1 km) trail with significant elevation changes. Another demanding option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1764682953/the-heart-of-the-catalan-pyrenees-la-fera-gravel" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The heart of the Catalan Pyrenees – La Fera gravel&lt;/a&gt;, which is a much longer, multi-day adventure.

Q: Can I find long-distance gravel biking options in the region?
A: Yes, La Pobla De Segur is a great starting point for longer gravel adventures. For instance, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1764682953/the-heart-of-the-catalan-pyrenees-la-fera-gravel" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The heart of the Catalan Pyrenees – La Fera gravel&lt;/a&gt; is an epic route spanning over 313 miles (500 km), offering a multi-day journey through the Pyrenees.

Q: Are there any circular gravel bike routes around La Pobla De Segur?
A: Absolutely. Many of the popular gravel routes in the area are loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38160925" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Coma Trail – View of Pantano de San Antonio loop from la Pobla de Segur&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40010131" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bartotrail – La Coma Trail loop from Acadèmia General Bàsica de Suboficials&lt;/a&gt;, which offer varied terrain and scenic views.

Q: What natural attractions can I see while gravel biking in La Pobla De Segur?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. Many routes follow the Noguera Pallaresa River and offer views of the Sant Antoni Reservoir. You can also find trails near the dramatic Collegats Gorge or leading to viewpoints of the Boumort mountain range. For specific natural highlights, consider visiting &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2698547" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;L'Argenteria Cliffs and Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; or the serene &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2472984" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake Montcortés&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any cultural or historical points of interest along the gravel trails?
A: Beyond natural landscapes, La Pobla de Segur offers cultural insights. The medieval old town itself is charming, and you might pass by modernist buildings like Torre Mauri Tower. While not directly on every trail, the region's history is evident, and you can explore sites like the Los Raiers Museum or the Romanesque Shrine of Sant Miquel in the wider area.

Q: What do other gravel bikers enjoy most about the trails in La Pobla De Segur?
A: The komoot community highly rates the gravel biking experience in La Pobla De Segur, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 14 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse terrain, from river valleys to mountain paths, and the stunning views of the Pyrenees, especially around the Sant Antoni Reservoir and the Noguera Pallaresa River.

Q: Is La Pobla De Segur suitable for family-friendly gravel cycling?
A: Yes, the region offers options for families. While some routes are challenging, there are flatter sections along the Noguera Pallaresa River and through agricultural plains that are more suitable for family outings. The Bernadot Nature Area also provides accessible natural spaces for shorter, relaxed excursions.

Q: What is the best time of year to go gravel biking in La Pobla De Segur?
A: The spring and autumn months generally offer the most pleasant conditions for gravel biking in La Pobla De Segur, with milder temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summers can be hot, especially in the valleys, while winters might bring colder temperatures and potential snow at higher elevations, though many lower-lying routes remain rideable.

Q: Are there any specific viewpoints or scenic spots accessible by gravel bike?
A: Many gravel routes in La Pobla De Segur are designed to showcase the region's panoramic beauty. Trails often lead to elevated positions offering expansive views of the confluence of the Noguera Pallaresa and Flamicell rivers, the Sant Antoni Reservoir, and the rugged Boumort mountain range. The red cliffs of Rocs de Queralt also provide striking geological vistas.

Q: Where can I find parking for gravel bike trailheads in La Pobla De Segur?
A: La Pobla De Segur, being a hub for outdoor activities, generally offers various parking options within the town and at common starting points for trails. Many routes, like those starting from La Pobla de Segur itself, will have accessible parking nearby. It's advisable to check local signage or specific route descriptions for the most convenient parking for your chosen trail.
tips:
The Castellàs track is to be enjoyed on two wheels, whether you are touring Pedals de Foc or another BTT route.

The landscape is unmatched and the type of terrain simple in this section.

The Pont de Suert is located at a historic crossroads.
The striking façade of the Pont de Suert church, the palace and its squares attract many lovers of Romanesque architecture.
Here you will find all the services you need if you are passing through traveling with your bicycle.
Vielha e Mijaran is the capital of the Vall d'Aran region, located in the old middle of the valley and which forms the nerve center of this region surrounded by large mountains.
In this area, the earth shows you its darkest colors and gives you the best panoramic views of the highest peaks. I invite you to park your bike and savor the incredible views that surround you.

Bagergue is considered one of the most beautiful villages in Spain. At 1,419 meters above sea level, it is the municipality located at the highest altitude in the Arán Valley. It belongs to the province of Lleida and has about 100 inhabitants.
Even if it is on the road, it is worth stopping for a moment and observing the surroundings. It is a real balcony.

From here you can enjoy stunning views and a perfect track.
In the event of a storm, you can use the Quatre Pins shelter to wait for it to pass.

Les Valls d'Aguilar is a municipality in the Alt Urgell region. It includes two decentralized municipal entities: the Guard of Ares and Taús. The valley is an entity that is quite isolated from the rest of the coves around it, creating a unique and wild space.

L'Argentería is a limestone rock wall located in the Collegats gorge, on the Noguera Pallaresa river. Its main value lies in the whimsical shapes that a water spring has modeled on the wall; it is said that Antonio Gaudí himself was inspired by its shapes for his designs.

waypoints.name: Views of the valley and the Torrassa reservoir
Spectacular section of the trail from Caregue to Espot
